source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane/Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Keathley Canyon Block 686

event.top_event: Eye injury from ejected indicator rod
actions_taken: Subsea technician lowered in personnel maintenance basket
Evacuation on next scheduled helicopter flight
Medical evaluation at Bourgeois Medical Clinic in Morgan City, Louisiana
BSEE Lafayette District Inspectors notified verbally at 08:00 hours on 7 August 2019
G-110 Incident of Noncompliance issued to LLOG
event.summary: On 7 August 2019 at 04:30 hours, a Seadrill subsea technician sustained an eye injury while working on the hands-free gooseneck (HFGN) during well operations. While attempting to retract the kill line HFGN locking pins, an indicator rod was ejected by trapped air and water pressure from within the operator housing, striking the technician in the right eye. The technician was diagnosed with orbital fractures and bruising. The incident resulted from operation of out-of-compliance equipment with known defects, failure to follow OEM guidelines, creation of unsafe workaround procedures, and failure to account for trapped hydrostatic pressure.
recommendations: BSEE recommends revising the bleed down procedures to accommodate for trapped pressure in choke and kill lines
BSEE Lafayette District recommends a Safety Alert be considered to address the hazards when unlocking the hands-free gooseneck choke and kill line assemblies

hazards.name: Trapped hydrostatic pressure in choke and kill lines

threats.name: Indicator rod ejection due to trapped pressure release
Operation of out-of-compliance equipment with known defects

consequences.name: Personnel eye injury and orbital fractures
Medical evacuation and lost work time

controls.name: OEM operational guidelines and procedures
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to ensure equipment compliance

supporting_text: Seadrill did not follow OEM operational guidelines since they knowingly operated the out of compliance equipment since December 2018
The equipment was outside the Original Equipment Manufactures (OEM) guidelines
The additional step with the known broken equipment was not performed within the OEM guidelines

controls.name: Equipment defect reporting and management of change
controls.barrier_role: Identify and document equipment defects to prevent unsafe operation
controls.lod_basis: Management system to track and resolve equipment issues

supporting_text: Seadrill documented the broken equipment case on an internal 'out of service' excel report dated on 17 December 2018
The equipment issue was not reported in a Synergi case
Seadrill personnel knew that the choke and kill indicator rods had been found to have sheared from the de-coupling segments

controls.name: Pressure bleed-down procedures for subsea equipment
controls.barrier_role: Prevent trapped pressure hazards during equipment maintenance
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to safely manage system pressures

supporting_text: The Seadrill team encountered unexpected trapped hydrostatic pressure in the choke and kill lines
The dangerous pressure was not accounted for during pressure tests
BSEE recommends revising the bleed down procedures to accommodate for the trapped pressure

controls.name: Personnel positioning and work planning during maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Reduce exposure to ejected objects and pressurized equipment
controls.lod_basis: Work planning to minimize personnel exposure to hazards

supporting_text: The Seadrill subsea technician was positioned in a personnel maintenance basket
As he was turning around in the personnel maintenance basket, the indicator rod was ejected
Improper body placement - The Seadrill subsea technician manually disengaged the locks to function the choke and kill stabs that placed him in the position to be struck by the sheared indicator rod

controls.name: Personal protective equipment (safety glasses)
controls.barrier_role: Reduce severity of eye injury from projectile impact
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to minimize injury severity

supporting_text: The indicator rod struck the Seadrill subsea technician in his right eye causing his safety glasses to be pushed back into his face contacting his eyebrow

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
